model_only.commentary: Schroders maintains a positive broad equity view because fundamentals remain supportive despite elevated volatility and near-term geopolitical risks. Evidence: Green Long/positive dot next to 'Equities'; text reads: 'We maintain our positive view on equities, fading near-term geopolitical risks. We recognise that volatility may remain elevated, however fundamentals remain supportive.'. Locator: p.2 — 'Main Asset Classes' view list (Our multi-asset investment views – March 2026).

Schroders remains underweight bonds because the level of yields is seen as a valuation risk to equities. Evidence: We have monetised our long commodity hedges but remain underweight bonds as the level of yields poses a valuation risk to equities.. Locator: p.2 (Our multi-asset investment views – March 2026).

Schroders expects the US 10-year yield to rise, which is an implied negative price view on intermediate US Treasuries. Evidence: Nevertheless, we think there is further upside in bond yields and would expect the US 10 year to rise to 4.5%.. Locator: p.2 (Our multi-asset investment views – March 2026).

Schroders closes its overweight to international value stocks because financials remain vulnerable to rate concerns and the US is expected to be more resilient. Evidence: We maintain our overweight position in equities but have decided to close the overweight to international value stocks as financials remain vulnerable to rate concerns and the US is likely to be more resilient to any protracted oil price spike.. Locator: p.2 (Our multi-asset investment views – March 2026).

Schroders is negative on government bonds because markets are viewed as too complacent about inflation risks from an extended Middle East conflict. Evidence: Red Short/negative dot next to 'Government bonds'; text reads: 'We remain negative on government bonds, reflecting the view that markets remain too complacent about the inflation risks of an extended conflict in the Middle East.'. Locator: p.2-p.3 — 'Main Asset Classes' view list (Our multi-asset investment views – March 2026).

Schroders downgraded broad credit to negative because tight spreads no longer reflect prevailing market risks. Evidence: Red Short/negative dot and Down from last month arrow next to 'Corporate bonds (credit)'; text reads: 'We have downgraded our score for credit to negative.'. Locator: p.3 — 'Main Asset Classes' view list (Our multi-asset investment views – March 2026).

Schroders downgraded EM USD investment grade credit because regional exposure to the Middle East and Asia raises spread-widening risk. Evidence: Red Short/negative dot and Down from last month arrow next to 'Emerging markets USD' under 'Investment grade credit'; text reads: 'We have downgraded our view, as regional exposure to the Middle East and Asia means the risk of spreads widening is greater than current levels currently reflect.'. Locator: p.5 — 'Investment grade credit' view list (Our multi-asset investment views – March 2026).

Schroders switches energy to neutral after taking profits, judging current price levels unsustainable while awaiting war-duration clarity. Evidence: Yellow Neutral dot and Down from last month arrow next to 'Energy'; text reads: 'We have taken profits on our energy position as oil prices have moved significantly higher since the start of the month.'. Locator: p.5 — 'Commodities' view list (Our multi-asset investment views – March 2026).

Schroders marks industrial metals neutral in the commodities view list, while noting potential aluminium supply disruption from the Middle East. Evidence: Yellow Neutral dot next to 'Industrial metals'; text begins: 'There is potential for supply side disruption to aluminium due to the Middle-East'. Locator: p.5 — 'Commodities' view list (Our multi-asset investment views – March 2026).

gt_only.source: Our multi-asset investment views – March 2026 | Quarterly markets review - Q1 2026
gt_only.commentary: Schroders maintains its positive view on equities, fading near-term geopolitical risks. While volatility may remain elevated due to the Middle East conflict, fundamentals remain supportive. The team has reduced cyclicality, closing the overweight to international value stocks as financials remain vulnerable to rate concerns. Low recession risk underpins the constructive stance.

gt_only.source: Our multi-asset investment views – March 2026 | Quarterly markets review - Q1 2026
gt_only.commentary: Downgraded EM USD debt. Regional exposure to Middle East and Asia means spread widening risk exceeds what current levels reflect.
